protocol.name: treeland_input_manager_unstable_v1
protocol.copyright: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2026 UnionTech Software Technology Co., Ltd. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

description.summary: configure input devices managed by treeland

protocol.description:
This protocol allows a privileged client to discover seat capabilities and configure compositor-managed mouse, touchpad, and keyboard devices.
A treeland_input_manager_v1 object is a global manager. It creates settings objects that represent configuration state for existing input devices; those settings objects are not the device objects themselves.
Mouse and touchpad settings are split into common pointer settings and device-specific settings. The lifetime of a settings object is independent from the wl_seat object used to identify the target at creation time.

interface.name: treeland_input_manager_v1
interface.description:
The treeland_input_manager_v1 interface is a global object used to create per-device settings objects for input devices exposed by the compositor.
The compositor advertises device capability changes with the capability_available and capability_unavailable events.
For each target device or seat-level settings scope, the compositor may allow at most one live settings object of a given interface created from one manager object. Creating a second settings object for the same target and interface is a protocol error.
virtual-pointer and virtual-keyboard are not included in the list.

entry.name: keyboard
entry.summary: keyboard input device, such as a standard keyboard

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroy this treeland_input_manager_v1 object.
Destroying the manager while child settings objects created from it are still alive is illegal and results in the protocol error.

request.name: get_pointer_settings
request.description:
Create a treeland_pointer_settings_v1 object for the specified seat.
The created object manages common pointer settings for the specified seat. These settings apply to pointer devices on that seat where the compositor supports the corresponding setting.
If the seat does not identify a valid target, or if it does not currently expose mouse or touchpad capability, the compositor raises the protocol error.

arg.name: seat
arg.summary: seat whose pointer settings are being managed

request.name: get_mouse_settings
request.description:
Create a treeland_mouse_settings_v1 object for the specified seat.
The created object manages mouse-specific settings that are not part of treeland_pointer_settings_v1.
The seat must currently expose mouse capability. If the seat does not identify a valid mouse-capable target, the compositor raises the protocol error.

arg.name: seat
arg.summary: seat whose mouse-specific settings are being managed

request.name: get_touchpad_settings
request.description:
Create a treeland_touchpad_settings_v1 object for the specified seat.
The created object manages touchpad-specific settings that are not part of treeland_pointer_settings_v1.
The seat must currently expose touchpad capability. If the seat does not identify a valid touchpad-capable target, the compositor raises the protocol error.

arg.name: seat
arg.summary: seat whose touchpad-specific settings are being managed

request.name: get_keyboard_settings
request.description:
Create a treeland_keyboard_settings_v1 object for the specified seat.
The seat must currently expose keyboard capability. If the seat does not identify a valid keyboard-capable target, the compositor raises the protocol error.

arg.name: seat
arg.summary: seat whose keyboard-specific settings are being managed

event.name: capability_available
event.description:
This event is emitted when the compositor detects that a seat now exposes the specified device capability.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_input_manager_v1 object is created to report capabilities currently available on a seat.
The compositor may also send this event later when input devices are hot-plugged and a seat gains the specified capability.
A client may create a treeland_mouse_settings_v1 object for a given seat only if the compositor has advertised device_type mouse capability for that seat. Likewise, a client may create a treeland_touchpad_settings_v1 or treeland_keyboard_settings_v1 object for a given seat only if the compositor has advertised device_type touchpad or keyboard capability for that seat.
If a seat has either mouse capability or touchpad capability, a client may create a treeland_pointer_settings_v1 object for that seat.
treeland_pointer_settings_v1 creating just one is enough.

event.name: capability_unavailable
event.description:
This event is emitted when the compositor detects that a seat no longer exposes the specified device capability.
The compositor may also send this event later when input devices are hot-unplugged and a seat loses the specified capability.
If type includes mouse capability, the client must destroy any treeland_mouse_settings_v1 objects targeting the seat.
If type includes touchpad capability, the client must destroy any treeland_touchpad_settings_v1 objects targeting the seat.
if a seat only had mouse (or only touchpad) capability, losing that single capability, the client must destroy treeland_pointer_settings_v1 objects targeting the seat.
If type includes keyboard capability, the client must destroy any treeland_keyboard_settings_v1 objects targeting the seat.

interface.name: treeland_pointer_settings_v1
interface.description:
The treeland_pointer_settings_v1 interface represents pending common pointer settings for a target seat.
Requests on this object update pending state. The compositor applies the accumulated settings when the client sends apply. Destroying the object before apply discards any pending state that has not yet been applied.
The compositor sends pointer state events after object creation to report the current effective pointer settings for the seat. It may later send additional state batches when those effective settings change for compositor-defined reasons. A done event terminates each event batch.

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroy this treeland_pointer_settings_v1 object.
Destroying the object discards any pending state that has not yet been applied.

request.name: set_scroll_factor

description.summary: set the scroll delta scaling factor

request.description:
Set a scaling factor applied to scroll axis deltas generated by wheel and other continuous scrolling input sources.
The compositor multiplies scroll axis values by the specified factor before forwarding them to clients. This affects continuous scroll deltas only and does not modify discrete scroll step events.
This request updates only the pending state associated with this object. The change takes effect after the next apply request.
Sending this request or the corresponding apply request does not by itself cause a scroll_factor event to be emitted.
deltas = factor * _deltas

arg.name: factor
arg.summary: scaling factor applied to scroll axis deltas

request.name: set_double_click_interval
request.description:
Set the double-click interval, in milliseconds. The change takes effect on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause a double_click_interval event to be emitted.

request.name: set_handed_mode
request.description:
Set whether primary and secondary buttons use left-handed or right-handed ordering. The change takes effect on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause a handed_mode event to be emitted.

request.name: apply
request.description: Ask the compositor to apply the pending pointer settings carried by this object.

event.name: scroll_factor
event.description:
This event reports the current scroll factor used for wheel and similar scroll input.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_pointer_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial pointer state batch.
Changes requested through set_scroll_factor do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: double_click_interval
event.description:
This event reports the current double-click interval, in milliseconds.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_pointer_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial pointer state batch.
Changes requested through set_double_click_interval do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: handed_mode
event.description:
This event reports whether left-handed or right-handed button ordering is currently used.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_pointer_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial pointer state batch.
Changes requested through set_handed_mode do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: failed
event.description:
This event indicates that the compositor failed to apply one or more pending pointer settings carried by this object.
This event does not destroy the object. The client may continue using the object and send further requests.

event.name: done

description.summary: end of a pointer state batch

event.description:
This event is sent once when all pointer state events in the current batch have been sent.
The compositor must always end a batch of pointer state events with this event, regardless of whether it sends the initial state or a later update.

interface.name: treeland_mouse_settings_v1
interface.description:
The treeland_mouse_settings_v1 interface represents pending mouse-specific settings for the all mouse associated with the target seat.
Requests on this object update pending state. The compositor applies the accumulated settings when the client sends apply. Destroying the object before apply discards any pending state that has not yet been applied.
The compositor sends mouse-specific state events after object creation to report the current effective settings for the target mouse. It may later send additional state batches when those effective settings change for compositor-defined reasons. A done event terminates each event batch.

enum.name: acceleration_profile
enum.description: Pointer acceleration profiles. The semantics correspond to libinput acceleration profiles.

entry.name: none
entry.summary: device does not have a configurable acceleration profile

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroy this treeland_mouse_settings_v1 object.
Destroying the object discards any pending state that has not yet been applied.

request.name: set_accel_speed
request.description:
Set the mouse acceleration speed. The change takes effect on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause an accel_speed event to be emitted.

request.name: set_acceleration_profile
request.description:
Set the acceleration profile. The change takes effect on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause an acceleration_profile event to be emitted.

request.name: set_natural_scroll
request.description:
Enable or disable natural scrolling. The change takes effect on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause a natural_scroll event to be emitted.

request.name: apply
request.description: Ask the compositor to apply the pending mouse-specific settings carried by this object.

event.name: accel_speed

description.summary: report the current mouse acceleration speed

event.description:
This event reports the current mouse acceleration speed.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_mouse_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial mouse state batch.
Changes requested through set_accel_speed do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: acceleration_profile
event.description:
This event reports the current acceleration profile used for the target mouse.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_mouse_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial mouse-specific state batch.
Changes requested through set_acceleration_profile do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: natural_scroll
event.description:
This event reports whether natural scrolling is enabled.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_mouse_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial pointer state batch.
Changes requested through set_natural_scroll do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: failed
event.description:
This event indicates that the compositor failed to apply one or more pending mouse-specific settings carried by this object.
This event does not destroy the object. The client may continue using the object and send further requests.

event.name: done

description.summary: end of a mouse-specific state batch

event.description:
This event is sent once when all mouse-specific state events in the current batch have been sent.
The compositor must always end a batch of mouse-specific state events with this event, regardless of whether it sends the initial state or a later update.

interface.name: treeland_touchpad_settings_v1
interface.description:
The treeland_touchpad_settings_v1 interface represents pending touchpad-specific settings for all touchpads associated with the target seat.
Requests on this object update pending state. The compositor applies the accumulated settings when the client sends apply. Destroying the object before apply discards any pending state that has not yet been applied.
The compositor sends touchpad-specific state events after object creation to report the current effective settings for the target seat. It may later send additional state batches when those effective settings change for compositor-defined reasons. A done event terminates each event batch.

entry.name: disable_while_typing
entry.summary: at least one touchpad supports disable-while-typing

entry.name: tap_to_click
entry.summary: at least one touchpad supports tap-to-click

enum.name: send_events_mode
enum.description: Modes controlling whether the target touchpad should send events and whether those events depend on the presence of an external pointer device on the same seat.

entry.name: enabled
entry.summary: send events from this touchpad normally

entry.name: disabled
entry.summary: do not send events through this touchpad.

entry.name: disabled_on_external_mouse
entry.summary: if an external pointer device is plugged in, do not send events from this touchpad; may be available on built-in touchpads

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroy this treeland_touchpad_settings_v1 object.
Destroying the object discards any pending state that has not yet been applied.

request.name: set_accel_speed
request.description:
Set the touchpad acceleration speed. The change takes effect on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause an accel_speed event to be emitted.

request.name: set_send_events_mode

description.summary: set the touchpad event delivery mode

request.description:
Set how events from the target touchpad are delivered. The change takes effect on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause a send_events_mode event to be emitted.

request.name: set_disable_while_typing
request.description:
Enable or disable temporarily disabling the seat's touchpads while the user is typing. The change takes effect on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause a disable_while_typing event to be emitted.

request.name: set_tap_to_click
request.description:
Enable or disable tap-to-click for all touchpads associated with the target seat. The change takes effect on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause a tap_to_click event to be emitted.

request.name: set_natural_scroll
request.description:
Enable or disable natural scrolling. The change takes effect on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause a natural_scroll event to be emitted.

request.name: apply
request.description: Ask the compositor to apply the pending touchpad-specific settings carried by this object.

event.name: feature
event.description:
This event reports which optional touchpad features are supported by at least one touchpad associated with the target seat.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_touchpad_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial touchpad-specific state batch.
The compositor may also send this event again when touchpad devices are added to or removed from the target seat and the set of supported features changes.

event.name: accel_speed

description.summary: report the current touchpad acceleration speed

event.description:
This event reports the current touchpad acceleration speed.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_touchpad_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial touchpad state batch.
Changes requested through set_accel_speed do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: send_events_mode

description.summary: report the current touchpad event delivery mode

event.description:
This event reports the current touchpad event delivery mode used for the target touchpad.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_touchpad_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial touchpad-specific state batch.
Changes requested through set_send_events_mode do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: disable_while_typing
event.description:
This event reports whether touchpads associated with the target seat are temporarily disabled while the user is typing.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_touchpad_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial touchpad-specific state batch.
Changes requested through set_disable_while_typing do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: tap_to_click
event.description:
This event reports whether tap-to-click is enabled for touchpads associated with the target seat.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_touchpad_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial touchpad-specific state batch.
Changes requested through set_tap_to_click do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: natural_scroll
event.description:
This event reports whether natural scrolling is enabled.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_touchpad_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial pointer state batch.
Changes requested through set_natural_scroll do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: failed
event.description:
This event indicates that the compositor failed to apply one or more pending touchpad-specific settings carried by this object.
This event does not destroy the object. The client may continue using the object and send further requests.

event.name: done

description.summary: end of a touchpad-specific state batch

event.description:
This event is sent once when all touchpad-specific state events in the current batch have been sent.
The compositor must always end a batch of touchpad-specific state events with this event, regardless of whether it sends the initial state or a later update.

interface.name: treeland_keyboard_settings_v1
interface.description:
The treeland_keyboard_settings_v1 interface represents pending settings for all keyboard associated with the target seat.
Requests on this object update pending keyboard state. The compositor applies the accumulated settings when the client sends apply.
The compositor sends keyboard-specific state events after object creation to report the current effective settings for the target seat. It may later send additional state batches when those effective settings change for compositor-defined reasons. A done event terminates each event batch.

request.name: destroy
request.description:
Destroy this treeland_keyboard_settings_v1 object.
Destroying the object discards any pending state that has not yet been applied.

request.name: set_repeat

description.summary: set keyboard repeat rate and delay

request.description:
Set the keyboard repeat rate and delay to apply on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause a repeat event to be emitted.

arg.name: rate
arg.summary: repeat rate in characters per second

arg.name: delay
arg.summary: delay in milliseconds before repeating starts

request.name: set_num_lock
request.description:
Set the num lock state to apply on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause a num_lock event to be emitted.

request.name: set_caps_lock_osd
request.description:
Enable or disable compositor-managed caps lock OSD feedback. The change takes effect on the next apply request.
This request updates only the pending state tracked by this object. Sending this request, or the following apply request, does not by itself cause a caps_lock_osd event to be emitted.
At present, the compositor does not implement functional caps lock OSD behavior for this setting. The value is only recorded as configuration state.

request.name: apply
request.description: Ask the compositor to apply the pending keyboard settings carried by this object.

event.name: repeat
event.description:
This event reports the current keyboard repeat configuration.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_keyboard_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial keyboard state batch.
Changes requested through set_repeat do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

arg.name: rate
arg.summary: repeat rate in characters per second

arg.name: delay
arg.summary: delay in milliseconds before repeating starts

event.name: num_lock

description.summary: report the current num lock state

event.description:
This event reports the current num lock state.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_keyboard_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial keyboard state batch.
Changes requested through set_num_lock do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: caps_lock_osd

description.summary: report the current caps lock OSD state

event.description:
This event reports whether compositor-managed caps lock OSD feedback is currently enabled.
The compositor sends this event immediately after the treeland_keyboard_settings_v1 object is created as part of the initial keyboard state batch.
Changes requested through set_caps_lock_osd do not cause this event to be emitted automatically.

event.name: failed
event.description:
This event indicates that the compositor failed to apply one or more pending keyboard settings carried by this object.
This event does not destroy the object. The client may continue using the object and send further requests.

event.name: done

description.summary: end of a keyboard state batch

event.description:
This event is sent once when all keyboard state events in the current batch have been sent.
The compositor must always end a batch of keyboard state events with this event, regardless of whether it sends the initial state or a later update.
